label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. So the problem gives me several time frames when Leslie was seen doing different things, and I need to find the time window when Leslie wasn't doing any of those things, which would be the possible time to go to the beach. Also, the beach closes after 10pm, so whatever time we pick has to be before 10pm. Let me list out all the known activities in order: 1. Leslie woke up at 5am. So before 5am, Leslie was asleep, but since the question is about the time Leslie could have gone to the beach, maybe the waking up time isn't directly relevant unless it's about when they became available. But probably, the activities that others saw are more important. 2. Emily saw Leslie in class from 5am to 9am. So Leslie was in class during that time. So Leslie couldn't have gone to the beach during 5am-9am. 3. Anthony saw Leslie watching a movie from 9am to 11am. So during 9am-11am, Leslie was at the theater. So that time window is taken up. 4. Thomas saw Leslie buying cookies from 11am to 4pm. So from 11am to 4pm, Leslie was at the bakery. So that's a long stretch of time where Leslie wasn't available. 5. Ashley saw Leslie fixing their computer from 4pm to 5pm. So during 4pm-5pm, Leslie was at the electronics store. 6. Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. So during 9pm-10pm, Leslie was on the way to the Statue of Liberty. Also, the beach was closed after 10pm. So the beach must have been open before 10pm. So Leslie would have had to go to the beach before 10pm. Now
